content.title: DEPRESSION
content.summary: {'A': '外界和非患者往往更难理解他人的心理疾病而不是身体疾病。尽管我们可能很容易同情那些承受身体疾病或残疾负担的人，但心理疾病往往带有污名，或者认为这些病症只存在于缺乏应对现实世界能力的人身上。现代生活的压力似乎导致情绪不和谐的案例增加，许多国家的政府最近的倡议都集中在增加公众对心理疾病和相关病症患者的意识和同情上。', 'B': '这段话主要讲了临床抑郁症的高发率和其可能成为全球第二大致残原因的预测，但也指出了一些专家对诊断标准的不准确性的质疑。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了许多人可能会经历低士气或情绪低落的时期，这是对生活中负面事件（如丧亲、失业或感情破裂）的自然人类反应。有些人甚至可能经历没有明显原因或触发器的抑郁期和低动力水平。临床抑郁症被归类为一种持续的消极状态，没有明显的原因，患者陷入持续消极思维的恶性循环，常常出现烦躁、持续疲劳和无精打采的症状。临床抑郁症患者据说比其他人更容易滥用药物甚至尝试自杀。', 'D': '这段话主要讲了临床抑郁症的诊断标准和症状表现。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了临床抑郁症的起因可能是多种生理和社会心理因素的组合，治疗方法因地区而异，但通常采用药物和心理疗法的组合，而在极端情况下，可能需要精神病院住院治疗。', 'F': '这段话主要讲了一些最近的研究认为，约四分之一被诊断为临床抑郁症的病例实际上应该被视为普通的悲伤和适应生活中的困难，这表明在这种情况下，需要心理治疗而不是药物治疗。心理治疗的康复过程通常比药物改善的速度更慢，但是一旦取得心理治疗的改善，观察到的长期和可持续性更好。各种咨询方法都旨在提高个体在个人和人际关系层面上的功能能力，会话包括鼓励个体以更积极的方式看待自己和他们的关系，以帮助患者用更积极的态度取代消极的想法。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了抑郁症易感性可能在家族中遗传，但尚不清楚是真正的遗传基因特征还是家庭成员共同的生物和环境因素导致的问题根源。在某些情况下，抑郁症患者可能需要摒弃一些已经在生活中形成的行为和态度，并发展新的应对策略，以帮助他们应对可能遇到的问题，消除他们在榜样中观察到并自己习得的破坏性行为模式。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: It is often more difficult for outsiders and non-sufferers to understand mental rather than physical illness in others. While it may be easy for us to sympathise with individuals living with the burden of a physical illness or disability, there is often a stigma attached to being mentally ill, or a belief that such conditions only exist in individuals who lack the strength of character to cope with the real world. The pressures of modern life seem to have resulted in an increase in cases of emotional disharmony and government initiatives in many countries have, of late, focussed on increasing the general public's awareness and sympathy towards sufferers of mental illness and related conditions.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Clinical depression, or 'major depressive disorder', a state of extreme sadness or despair, is said to affect up to almost 20% of the population at some point in their lives prior to the age of 40. Studies have shown that this disorder is the leading cause of disability in North America; in the UK almost 3 million people are said to be diagnosed with some form of depression at any one time, and experts believe that as many as a further 9 million other cases may go undiagnosed. World Health Organisation projections indicate that clinical depression may become the second most significant cause of disability' on a global scale by 2020. However, such figures are not unanimously supported, as some experts believe that the diagnostic criteria used to identify the condition are not precise enough, leading to other types of depression being wrongly classified as 'clinical'.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
Many of us may experience periods of low morale or mood and feelings of dejection, as a natural human response to negative events in our lives such as bereavement, redundancy or breakdown of a relationship. Some of us may even experience periods of depression and low levels of motivation which have no tangible reason or trigger. Clinical depression is classified as an on-going state of negativity, with no tangible cause, where sufferers enter a spiral of persistent negative thinking, often experiencing irritability, perpetual tiredness and listlessness.
Sufferers of clinical depression are said to be at higher risk of resorting to drug abuse or even suicide attempts than the rest of the population.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Clinical depression is generally diagnosed when an individual is observed to exhibit an excessively depressed mood and/or 'anhedonia' – an inability to experience pleasure from positive experiences such as enjoying a meal or pleasurable social interaction – for a period of two weeks or more, in conjunction with five or more additional recognised symptoms. These additional symptoms may include overwhelming feelings of sadness; inability to sleep, or conversely, excessive sleeping; feelings of guilt, nervousness, abandonment or fear; inability to concentrate; interference with memory capabilities; fixation with death or extreme change in eating habits and associated weight gain or loss.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Clinical depression was originally solely attributed to chemical imbalance in the brain, and while anti-depressant drugs which work to optimise levels of 'feel good' chemicals – serotonin and norepinephrine – are still commonly prescribed today, experts now believe that onset of depression may be caused by a number, and often combination of, physiological and socio-psychological factors. Treatment approaches vary quite dramatically from place to place and are often tailored to an individual's particular situation; however, some variation of a combination of medication and psychotherapy is most commonly used. The more controversial electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) may also be used where initial approaches fail. In extreme cases, where an individual exhibits behaviour which Indicates that they may cause physical harm to themselves, psychiatric hospitalisation may be necessary as a form of intensive therapy.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Some recent studies, such as those published by the Archives of General Psychiatry, hold that around a quarter of diagnosed clinical depression cases should actually be considered as significant but none-the-less ordinary sadness and maladjustment to coping with trials in life, indicating that in such cases, psychotherapy rather than treatment through medication is required. Recovery as a result of psychotherapy tends, in most cases, to be a slower process than improvements related to medication; however, improvements as a result of psychological treatment, once achieved, have been observed in some individuals to be more long term and sustainable than those attained through prescription drugs. Various counselling approaches exist, though all focus on enhancing the subject's ability to function on a personal and interpersonal level. Sessions involve encouragement of an individual to view themselves and their relationships in a more positive manner, with the intention of helping patients to replace negative thoughts with a more positive outlook.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: It is apparent that susceptibility to depression can run in families. However, it remains unclear as to whether this is truly an inherited genetic trait or whether biological and environmental factors common to family members may be at the root of the problem. In some cases, sufferers of depression may need to unlearn certain behaviours and attitudes they have established in life and develop new coping strategies designed to help them deal with problems they may encounter, undoing patterns of destructive behaviour they may have observed in their role models and acquired for themselves.

questions.title: Details of treatment alternatives for worst case scenario depression.
questions.analysis:
题干中提到了"Details of treatment alternatives for worst case scenario depression."，我们需要找到与之相关的段落。在原文中，我们可以找到以下几个关键信息：
1. "Treatment approaches vary quite dramatically from place to place and are often tailored to an individual’s particular situation; however, some variation of a combination of medication and psychotherapy is most commonly used." 这句话提到了治疗方法的变化和个体化的情况，包括药物和心理治疗的结合使用。
2. "The more controversial electroconvulsive therapy (ECT) may also be used where initial approaches fail." 这句话提到了当初始治疗方法失败时，可能会使用更具争议的电抽搐疗法（ECT）。
3. "In extreme cases, where an individual exhibits behaviour which Indicates that they may cause physical harm to themselves, psychiatric hospitalisation may be necessary as a form of intensive therapy." 这句话提到了在极端情况下，当个体表现出可能对自己造成身体伤害的行为时，精神病院住院可能是一种强化治疗的形式。

根据题干中的"Details of treatment alternatives for worst case scenario depression."，我们可以看出这是在讨论最严重的抑郁症情况下的治疗选择。而根据以上三个段落，我们可以得出结论，这些段落都提到了与抑郁症治疗相关的信息，因此本题对应的段落为E段落。

questions.analysis:
题干中提到了关于未来病例增长的估计为什么可能过于夸大的信息。我们可以在原文中找到相关的句子来定位答案。

首先，题干中提到了"estimates of incidence of future growth"，这与原文中的"figures"相对应。在原文中，有一句话提到了这个问题："However, such figures are not unanimously supported, as some experts believe that the diagnostic criteria used to identify the condition are not precise enough, leading to other types of depression being wrongly classified as ‘clinical’." 这句话表明有些专家认为诊断标准不够准确，导致其他类型的抑郁症被错误地归类为“临床抑郁症”。这说明了对未来病例增长的估计可能被夸大。

其次，题干中提到了"overly exaggerated"，这与原文中的"not unanimously supported"相对应。同样，在上述句子中，"such figures are not unanimously supported"表明这些数据并没有得到一致的支持，也就是说有些人并不认同这些数据。

综上所述，根据题干中的信息和原文中的相关句子，我们可以得出结论：本题对应的段落是B段落。

collect_option.title: Which THREE of the following statements are true of depression?

list.text: Governments have generally failed to take action to educate the general public about the condition.
The highest reported number of cases are in the USA.
In Britain, it is likely that there are more individuals who live with the condition without the help of a doctor than those being officially treated.
Clinical depression may be triggered by divorce.
Lethargy may be one of the symptoms of depression.
Prescribed pharmaceuticals have radically changed over recent years.
Approaches to treating depression are not universal.

questions.analysis:
这道题要求选择三个关于抑郁症的正确陈述。我们可以通过以下步骤快速找到答案：

首先，我们需要找到与抑郁症相关的信息。在原文中，抑郁症被称为"clinical depression"。在题干中，我们可以看到"depression"这个词，这是一个定位词。

接下来，我们需要找到与题干中的每个选项相关的信息。让我们逐个分析：

1. "In Britain, it is likely that there are more individuals who live with the condition without the help of a doctor than those being officially treated."（在英国，可能有更多的人在没有医生帮助的情况下生活，而不是接受官方治疗。）

在原文中，我们可以找到类似的信息：“Clinical depression was originally solely attributed to chemical imbalance in the brain, and while anti-depressant drugs which work to optimise levels of ‘feel good’ chemicals – serotonin and norepinephrine – are still commonly prescribed today, experts now believe that onset of depression may be caused by a number, and often combination of, physiological and socio-psychological factors.”（临床抑郁症最初被认为仅仅是大脑中化学物质不平衡的结果，尽管目前仍然常规开处方的抗抑郁药物可以优化“感觉良好”的化学物质——血清素和去甲肾上腺素的水平，但专家们现在认为，抑郁症的发作可能是由多种生理和社会心理因素的组合引起的。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

2. "Lethargy may be one of the symptoms of depression."（倦怠可能是抑郁症的症状之一。）

在原文中，我们找到了类似的信息：“Clinical depression was originally solely attributed to chemical imbalance in the brain, and while anti-depressant drugs which work to optimise levels of ‘feel good’ chemicals – serotonin and norepinephrine – are still commonly prescribed today, experts now believe that onset of depression may be caused by a number, and often combination of, physiological and socio-psychological factors.”（临床抑郁症最初被认为仅仅是大脑中化学物质不平衡的结果，尽管目前仍然常规开处方的抗抑郁药物可以优化“感觉良好”的化学物质——血清素和去甲肾上腺素的水平，但专家们现在认为，抑郁症的发作可能是由多种生理和社会心理因素的组合引起的。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

3. "Approaches to treating depression are not universal."（治疗抑郁症的方法并不普遍。）

在原文中，我们找到了类似的信息：“Treatment approaches vary quite dramatically from place to place and are often tailored to an individual’s particular situation; however, some variation of a combination of medication and psychotherapy is most commonly used.”（治疗方法在不同地方有很大的差异，并且通常根据个人的特定情况进行调整；然而，通常使用的是一种药物和心理治疗的组合的变体。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

综上所述，正确的选项是：
"In Britain, it is likely that there are more individuals who live with the condition without the help of a doctor than those being officially treated."
"Lethargy may be one of the symptoms of depression."
"Approaches to treating depression are not universal."

questions.analysis:
这道题要求选择三个关于抑郁症的正确陈述。我们可以通过以下步骤快速找到答案：

首先，我们需要找到与抑郁症相关的信息。在原文中，抑郁症被称为"clinical depression"。在题干中，我们可以看到"depression"这个词，这是一个定位词。

接下来，我们需要找到与题干中的每个选项相关的信息。让我们逐个分析：

1. "In Britain, it is likely that there are more individuals who live with the condition without the help of a doctor than those being officially treated."（在英国，可能有更多的人在没有医生帮助的情况下生活，而不是接受官方治疗。）

在原文中，我们可以找到类似的信息：“Clinical depression was originally solely attributed to chemical imbalance in the brain, and while anti-depressant drugs which work to optimise levels of ‘feel good’ chemicals – serotonin and norepinephrine – are still commonly prescribed today, experts now believe that onset of depression may be caused by a number, and often combination of, physiological and socio-psychological factors.”（临床抑郁症最初被认为仅仅是大脑中化学物质不平衡的结果，尽管目前仍然常规开处方的抗抑郁药物可以优化“感觉良好”的化学物质——血清素和去甲肾上腺素的水平，但专家们现在认为，抑郁症的发作可能是由多种生理和社会心理因素的组合引起的。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

2. "Lethargy may be one of the symptoms of depression."（倦怠可能是抑郁症的症状之一。）

在原文中，我们找到了类似的信息：“Clinical depression was originally solely attributed to chemical imbalance in the brain, and while anti-depressant drugs which work to optimise levels of ‘feel good’ chemicals – serotonin and norepinephrine – are still commonly prescribed today, experts now believe that onset of depression may be caused by a number, and often combination of, physiological and socio-psychological factors.”（临床抑郁症最初被认为仅仅是大脑中化学物质不平衡的结果，尽管目前仍然常规开处方的抗抑郁药物可以优化“感觉良好”的化学物质——血清素和去甲肾上腺素的水平，但专家们现在认为，抑郁症的发作可能是由多种生理和社会心理因素的组合引起的。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

3. "Approaches to treating depression are not universal."（治疗抑郁症的方法并不普遍。）

在原文中，我们找到了类似的信息：“Treatment approaches vary quite dramatically from place to place and are often tailored to an individual’s particular situation; however, some variation of a combination of medication and psychotherapy is most commonly used.”（治疗方法在不同地方有很大的差异，并且通常根据个人的特定情况进行调整；然而，通常使用的是一种药物和心理治疗的组合的变体。）

所以，这个选项是正确的。

综上所述，正确的选项是：
"In Britain, it is likely that there are more individuals who live with the condition without the help of a doctor than those being officially treated."
"Lethargy may be one of the symptoms of depression."
"Approaches to treating depression are not universal."

content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了建筑是关于演变而非革命的，罗马人在五世纪撤离英国后，他们优雅的别墅、精心规划的城镇和哈德良长城等工程奇迹似乎只是随着英国文化陷入黑暗时代而衰败。直到1066年的诺曼征服才重新带来光明，中世纪的哥特式大教堂建筑师在英国文化复兴中起到了重要作用。然而，事实并不像那样简单，罗马不列颠文化（包括建筑、语言、宗教、政治组织和艺术）在罗马撤军后仍然存活了很长时间。尽管盎格鲁-撒克逊人有自己复杂的建筑风格，但很少有建筑物留存下来证明他们的成就，因为绝大多数盎格鲁-撒克逊建筑都是木质的。', 'B': '中世纪建筑注重实用性，而16世纪建筑则更注重展示地位和财富。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了文艺复兴时期建筑的特点，包括更加注重外观装饰、房间更大更亮、使用大面积玻璃等。', 'D': '17世纪早期的建筑大多延续了晚期都铎时期的天真热情，直到英国内战后，贵族们在大陆接触到法国、荷兰和意大利的建筑风格，随着查理二世的复辟，英国巴洛克风格的建筑活动迅猛发展，展现了权威的再次确立和绝对主义思想的表达，尽管其政治基础值得质疑，但其作品令人叹为观止。', 'E': '19世纪的建筑包含了两种不同的风格，一种是追求新工业工艺的大型玻璃和铁制水晶宫，另一种是反对工业化的改革者如约翰·拉斯金和威廉·莫里斯，他们努力回归手工制造技术。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了20世纪初英国错过了现代建筑的重要趋势，而在这个时期，德国的格罗皮乌斯和法国的勒·柯布西耶等建筑师正在尝试使用新材料和新技术，而英国的建筑师们仍然坚守传统，为过时的地主阶级设计新古典主义和文艺复兴风格的乡村别墅。只有少数几座具有真正价值的现代主义建筑在20世纪20年代和30年代在英国建成，而其中大部分是由定居在英国的外国建筑师如谢尔马耶夫、卢贝金和戈德芬格等完成的。\n\n简短总结：英国错过了20世纪初现代建筑的重要趋势，只有少数外国建筑师在这里建造了一些有价值的现代主义建筑。', 'G': '二战后，随着英国的重建工作开始，现代主义运动的信仰与未来的理念与战后英国的情绪产生共鸣，因此迫切需要能够快速生产的廉价住房，这导致了对预制元素、金属框架、混凝土装饰以及缺乏装饰的采用，这些都是被国外现代主义者所接受但在英国受到怀疑的。地方政府成为了建筑的重要赞助者，这标志着建筑界的主导地位从几个世纪以来一直由私人个体所主导的情况发生了转变。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了英国建筑领域的发展历程，从战后开始，地方政府、国家和跨国公司以及大型教育机构主导了英国建筑，而到了20世纪80年代末，现代主义因被不公平地归咎于高层住宅中的社会实验而败给了后现代主义，后现代主义以其从任何地方和任何时期借鉴的欢快特点取而代之，但现在，在新千年时代，甚至后现代主义也显示出老化的迹象，接下来会是什么呢？后后现代主义？'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Architecture is about evolution, not revolution. It used to be thought that once the Romans pulled out of Britain in the fifth century, their elegant villas, carefully-planned towns and engineering marvels like Hadrian's Wall simply fell into decay as British culture was plunged into the Dark Ages. It took the Norman Conquest of 1066 to bring back the light, and the othic cathedral-builders of the Middle Ages played an important part in the revival of British culture. However, the truth is not as simple as that Romano-British culture – and that included architecture along with language, religion, political organization and the arts – survived long after the Roman withdrawal. And although the Anglo- Saxons had a sophisticated building style of their own, little survives to bear witness to their achievements as the vast majority of Anglo-Saxon buildings were made of wood.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Even so, the period between the Norman landing at Pevensey in 1066 and the day in 1485 when Richard III lost his horse and his head at Bosworth, ushering in the Tudors and the /Early Modern period, marks a rare flowering of British building. And it is all the more remarkable because the underlying ethos of medieval architecture was 'fitness for purpose'. The great cathedrals and parish churches that lifted up their towers to heaven were not only acts of devotion in stone; they were also fiercely functional buildings. Castles served their particular purpose and their battlements and turrets were for use rather than ornament. In a sense, the buildings of the 16th century were also governed by fitness for purpose – only now, the purpose was very different. In domestic architecture, in particular, buildings were used to display status and wealth.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: This stately and curious workmanship showed itself in various ways. A greater sense of security led to more outward-looking buildings, as opposed to the medieval arrangement where the need for defense created houses that faced inward onto a courtyard or series of courtyards. This allowed for much more in the way of exterior ornament. The rooms themselves tended to be bigger and lighter – as an expensive commodity, the use of great expanses of glass was in itself a statement of wealth. There was also a general move towards balanced and symmetrical exteriors with central entrances.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: With the exception of Inigo Jones (1573-1652), whose confident handling of classical detail and proportion set him apart from all other architects of the period, most early 17th century buildings tended to take the innocent exuberance of late Tudor work one step further. /But during the 1640s and 50s the Civil War and its aftermath sent many gentlemen and nobles to the Continent either to escape the fighting or, when the war was lost, to follow Charles II into exile. There they came into contact with French, Dutch and Italian architecture and, with Charles's restoration in 1660, there was a flurry of building activity as royalists reclaimed their property and built themselves houses reflecting the latest European trends. The British Baroque was a reassertion of authority, an expression of absolutist ideology by men who remembered a world turned upside down during the Civil War. The style is heavy and rich, sometimes overblown and melodramatic. The politics which underpin it are questionable, but its products are breathtaking.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The huge glass-and-iron Crystal Palace, designed by Joseph Paxton to house the Great Exhibition of 1851, shows another strand to 19th century architecture – one which embraced new industrial processes. But it wasn't long before even this confidence in progress came to be regarded with suspicion. Mass production resulted in buildings and furnishings that were too perfect, as the individual craftsman no longer had a major role in their creation. Railing against the dehumanising effects of industrialisation, reformers like John Ruskin and William Morris made a concerted effort to return to hand-crafted, pre-industrial manufacturing techniques.Morris's influence grew from the production of furniture and textiles, until by the 1880s a generation of principled young architects was following his call for good, honest construction.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The most important trends in early 20th century architecture simply passed Britain by. Whilst Gropius was working on cold, hard expanses of glass, and Le Corbusier was experimenting with the use of reinforced concrete frames, we had staid establishment architects like Edwin Lutyens producing Neo-Georgian and Renaissance country houses for an outmoded landed class. In addition there were slightly batty architect-craftsmen, the heirs of William Morris, still trying to turn the clock back to before the Industrial Revolution by making chairs and spurning new technology. Only a handful of Modern Movement buildings of any real merit were produced here during the 1920s and 1930s, and most of these were the work of foreign architects such as Serge Chermayeff, Berthold Lubetkin and Erno Goldf inger who had settled in this country.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: After the Second World War the situation began to change. The Modern Movement's belief in progress and the future struck a chord with the mood of post-war Britain and, as reconstruction began under Attlee's Labour government in 1945, there was a desperate need for cheap housing which could be produced quickly. The use of prefabricated elements, metal frames, concrete cladding and the absence of decoration – all of which had been embraced by Modernists abroad and viewed with suspicion by the British -were adopted to varying degrees for housing developments and schools. Local authorities, charged with the task of rebuilding city center, became important patrons of architecture. This represented a shift away from the private individuals who had dominated the architectural scene for centuries.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Since the War it has been corporate bodies like these local authorities, together with national and multinational companies, and large educational institutions, which have dominated British architecture. By the late 1980s the Modern Movement, unfairly blamed for the social experiments implicit in high- rise housing, had lost out to irony and spectacle in the shape of post-modernism, with its cheerful borrowings from anywhere and any period. But now, in the new Millennium, even post-modernism is showing signs of age. What comes next? Post-post-modernism?

content.title: Compliance Or Noncompliance For Children
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲了社会化是一个漫长的过程，而顺从是其起点，因此对于儿童教育来说，顺从是首要的。同时，研究发现母子之间的积极情感和母亲的控制是儿童内化的相关因素，而积极情感的共享与高水平的顺从和内化有关。此外，研究还探讨了儿童对请求和禁令的顺从之间的差异和相似之处，发现对于幼儿来说，母亲的请求比禁令更具挑战性。最后，文章讨论了顺从对于行为规范的内化的影响。\n\n简短总结：社会化的起点是顺从，母子之间的积极情感和母亲的控制是儿童内化的相关因素，顺从对于行为规范的内化具有重要影响。', 'B': '这段话主要讲了父母的管教方式对孩子的服从和不服从行为的影响，发现爱的撤离结合其他技巧是最有效的，而身体强制相对较少有效，理性和口头禁止则完全无效，除非与身体强制结合使用。', 'C': '非服从的孩子在成长过程中可能会学会使用更高级的方法来表达他们的不服从，他们更善于通过谈判或给出拒绝的理由来表达自己的反对意见，而不是直接向父母表达。', 'D': '通过安排日常活动，将经常被拒绝的活动安排在高度偏好的活动之前，可以消除反抗行为并激励孩子去做不喜欢的活动，但不是以只有完成不喜欢的活动才能进行喜欢的活动的方式呈现，而是通过措辞让孩子认为在进行喜欢的活动之前必须先完成不喜欢的活动。', 'E': '这段话主要讲了表扬是让孩子遵守规则的最好方法，但需要知道如何正确地表扬以鼓励孩子今后继续良好行为。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Many Scientists believe that socialization takes a long process, while compliance is the outset of it. Accordingly, compliance for the education of children is the priority. Motivationally distinct forms of child compliance, mutually positive affect, and maternal control, observed in 3 control contexts in 103 dyads of mothers and their 26-41-month-old children, were examined as correlates of internalization, assessed using observations of children while alone with prohibited temptations and maternal ratings. One form of compliance (committed compliance), when the child appeared committed wholeheartedly to the maternal agenda and eager to endorse and accept it, was emphasized. Mother-child mutually positive affect was both a predictor and a concomitant of committed compliance. Children who shared positive effect with their mothers showed a high level of committed compliance and were also more internalized. Differences and similarities between children's compliance with requests and prohibitions ("Do"vs. "Don't" demand contexts) were also explored. Maternal "Dos" appeared more challenging to toddlers than the "Don'ts." Some individual coherence of behavior was also found across both demand contexts. The implication of committed compliance for emerging internalized regulators of conduct is discussed.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: A number of parents were not easy to be aware of the compliance, some even overlooked their children's noncompliance. Despite good education, these children did not follow the words from their parents on several occasion, especially boys in certain ages. Fortunately, this rate was acceptable, some parents could be patient with the noncompliance. Someone held that noncompliance is probably not a wrong thing. In order to determine the effects of different parental disciplinary techniques on young children's compliance and noncompliance, mothers were trained to observe emotional incidents involving their own toddler-aged children. Reports of disciplinary encounters were analyzed in terms of the types of discipline used (reasoning, verbal prohibition, physical coercion, love withdrawal, and combinations thereof) and children's responses to that discipline (compliance/ noncompliance and avoidance). The relation between compliance/ noncompliance and type of misdeed (harm to persons, harm to property, and lapses of self-control) was also analyzed. Results indicated that love withdrawal combined with other techniques was most effective in securing children's compliance and that its effectiveness was not a function of the type of technique with which it was combined. Avoidant responses and affective reunification with the parent were more likely to follow love withdrawal than any other technique. Physical coercion was somewhat less effective than love withdrawal, while reasoning and verbal prohibition were not at all effective except when both were combined with physical coercion.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text:
"Noncompliant Children sometimes prefer to say to directly as they were younger, they are easy to deal with the relationship with contemporaries. When they are growing up. During the period that children are getting elder, who may learn to use more advanced approaches for their noncompliance. They are more skillful to negotiate or give reasons for refusal rather than show their opposite idea to parents directly." Said Henry Porter, a scholar working in
Psychology Institute of UK. He indicated that noncompliance means growth in some way, may have benefit for children. Many Experts held different viewpoints in recent years, they tried drilling compliance into children. His collaborator Wallace Friesen believed that Organizing a child's daily activities so that they occur in the same order each day as much as possible. This first strategy for defiant children is ultimately the most important. Developing a routine helps a child to know what to expect and increases the chances that he or she will comply with things such as chores, homework, and hygiene requests. When undesirable activities occur in the same order at optimal times during the day, they become habits that are not questioned but done without thought.
Chances are that you have developed some type of routine for yourself in terms of showering, cleaning your house, or doing other types of work. You have an idea in your mind when you will do these things on a regular basis and this helps you to know what to expect. In fact, you have probably already been using most of these compliance strategies for yourself without realizing it. For children, without setting these expectations on a daily basis by making them part of a regular routine, they can become very upset. Just like adults, children think about what they plan to do that day and expect to be able to do what they want. So, when you come along and ask them to do something they weren't already planning to do that day, this can result in automatic refusals and other undesirable defiant behaviors. However, by using this compliance strategy with defiant children, these activities are done almost every day in the same general order and the child expects to already do them.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Doctor Steven Walson addressed that organizing fun activities to occur after frequently refused activities. This strategy also works as a positive reinforcer when the child complies with your requests. By arranging your day so that things often refused to occur right before highly preferred activities, you are able to eliminate defiant behavior and motivate your child's behavior of doing the undesirable activity. This is not to be presented in a way that the preferred activity is only allowed if a defiant child does the non-preferred activity. However, you can word your request in a way so that your child assumes that you have to do the non-preferred activity before moving on to the next preferred activity. For example, you do not want to say something such as, "If you clean your room we can play a game." Instead of the word your request like this, "As soon as you are done cleaning your room we will be able to play that really fun game you wanted to play."

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Psychologist Paul Edith insisted praise is the best way to make children comply with. This is probably a common term you are used to hearing by now. If you praise your child's behavior, he or she will be more likely to do that behavior. So, it is essential to use praise when working with defiant children. It also provides your child with positive attention. However, it is important to know how to praise children in a way that encourages future automatic reinforcement for your child when doing a similar behavior.
